matter.title: Sale of 129 Old Mill Road from Grace C. Roe to Maria Q. Fairbanks

jurisdiction.court_type: Maine Registry of Deeds / Maine Revenue Services

matter.summary: Maria Q. Fairbanks is purchasing residential condominium at 129 Old Mill Road, Portland, Maine from Grace C. Roe for $831933, requiring a Maine Real Estate Transfer Tax Declaration at recording.

fact_pattern.summary: Maria Q. Fairbanks is purchasing residential condominium at 129 Old Mill Road, Portland, Maine from Grace C. Roe for $831933, requiring a Maine Real Estate Transfer Tax Declaration at recording.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe has agreed to sell, and Maria Q. Fairbanks to buy, the residential condominium property at 129 Old Mill Road in Portland, Sagadahoc County, Maine. The agreed purchase price is $831933, with closing set for about 2026-03-17.

The buyer is financing the purchase through conventional mortgage, and title will pass by quitclaim deed with covenant. At recording in the Sagadahoc County Registry of Deeds, the parties must file a Real Estate Transfer Tax Declaration (RETTD) reporting the price, the property, and the transferor and transferee, and pay the Maine transfer tax unless an exemption applies.

Counsel is handling the closing, confirming clear title, prorating taxes, and preparing the deed and the transfer tax declaration for the parties' signatures.
undisputed_facts: The property at 129 Old Mill Road, Portland is being transferred from Grace C. Roe to Maria Q. Fairbanks.
The transfer must be reported on a Maine RETTD at recording.
disputed_facts: Whether a transfer tax exemption applies to this transaction.
The allocation of prorated property taxes and closing costs between the parties.

third_parties.name: County Registry of Deeds
third_parties.relevance: Records the deed and the transfer tax declaration.

third_parties.name: Lighthouse Partners Corp.
third_parties.relevance: Issues the owner's and lender's title policies at closing.

exchanges.question: What are you buying and for how much?
exchanges.answer: The residential condominium at 129 Old Mill Road in Portland, for $831933.

exchanges.question: How are you paying?
exchanges.answer: Through conventional mortgage. We expect to close around 2026-03-17.

exchanges.question: Any known title concerns?
exchanges.answer: Not that we know of; we are getting title insurance and a quitclaim deed with covenant.

exchanges.question: Do you understand the transfer tax?
exchanges.answer: We know there is a Maine transfer tax reported on the RETTD at recording, split between buyer and seller.

exchanges.question: What do you need from the closing?
exchanges.answer: Clear title, a clean RETTD, and the deed recorded correctly.

goals: Close the purchase with clear, insurable title.
File an accurate Real Estate Transfer Tax Declaration and pay the correct tax.
Record the deed promptly in the county registry.
desired_outcomes: A recorded deed and a properly filed RETTD.
non_starters: Closing with an unresolved title defect.

constraints.budget: Standard closing costs; buyer and seller split the transfer tax.

issues.issue: Calculation of the Maine real estate transfer tax
sub_questions: What is the consideration subject to tax?
How is the tax split between transferor and transferee?
Are there liens, easements, or encumbrances of record?

evidence.title: Property tax assessment
evidence.description: The municipal assessment supporting adjusted assessed value.

evidence.title: Settlement statement
evidence.description: Closing disclosure with prorations and the transfer tax.

evidence.title: Title commitment
evidence.description: The title company's commitment and exceptions.

evidence.title: Proposed deed
evidence.description: The deed prepared for the conveyance.

amounts.label: Estimated Maine transfer tax
amounts.basis: $2.20 per $500 of value, split between the parties.
